branch_metadata.branch_rationale: System-R coefficient derivation / search-space audit; branch-blind algebraic test

description: Coefficient-perturbation null. Replaces the five measured causal-wave coefficients with random uniform draws from [0.02, 0.99]^5 and re-evaluates the three EXACT-tier landing forms. Reports the probability that random coefficients reproduce all three EXACT hits or at least one.

rng_provenance.rng_implementation_upstream: numpy.random.default_rng (full enumeration null over the 222954-element search space)
rng_provenance.rng_implementation_local: stdlib random.Random (rigid-baseline-formula null only; complements but does not reproduce the upstream)
rng_provenance.comment: TWO DISTINCT NULL STATISTICS, both reported here. (a) The cached p_all_3_targets_hit_under_random_coefficients = 0.885 is produced UPSTREAM by enumerating all 222954 algebraic compositions per random coefficient draw (full enumeration null); this is the look-elsewhere-relevant number quoted in the abstract. (b) The on-the-fly regenerator at src/perturb_coefficients_null.py evaluates ONLY the rigid baseline formulas of L6/L7/L8 (no enumeration); under --seed 0 --trials 200 it returns p_all_three approximately 0 (random rigid formulas almost never hit the EXACT cut). The on-the-fly script is the conservative-companion null. The local regression test tests/test_null_models.py::test_perturbation_null_runs_with_small_seed asserts the rigid-formula null hit-rate stays below 0.10. A separate test tests/test_null_models.py::test_null_does_not_reproduce_robust_core asserts the random-coefficient L1-L5 PRECISE_2.5 closure rate is below 0.10 (a different statistic). Reproduction of the cached 0.885 requires the full enumeration null pipeline upstream of this repo.

interpretation: Random coefficient sets reproduce all three EXACT hits 88.5% of the time and at least one 100% of the time; the EXACT classification of rows L6, L7, L8 is therefore look-elsewhere-caveated and is NOT a standalone structural proof of the underlying transport law. The robust claim is the PRECISE_2.5-or-better closure on rows L1-L5.
